description: Tests skill invocation on real BLE project prompts. Positive cases: plan/implement/modify/add keywords, or any plan involving new logic. Negative cases: read-only, explain, search, build ops.

evals.prompt: /plan Add a safe_divide(uint32_t a, uint32_t b) function that returns a / b, or 0 if b is zero.
evals.expected_output: A plan that includes a preflight report covering call graph, arithmetic ranges, and freed-resource access as a section before the edit steps.

assertions.text: Response includes a '## Preflight:' section header or 'Preflight safe_divide:' shorthand
assertions.description: Skill must emit a preflight report when planning new function logic

assertions.text: Response includes an 'Arithmetic:' check in the preflight output
assertions.description: Division-by-zero is the key arithmetic risk; skill should explicitly clear or flag it

assertions.text: The preflight section appears before any code or edit steps
assertions.description: Skill specifies: emit the report before describing what you will write

evals.prompt: /plan Explain what connection_find does and list its callers.
evals.expected_output: A read-only explanation of the function — no preflight block.

assertions.text: Response does NOT include a '## Preflight:' section or 'Preflight connection_find:' line
assertions.description: Read-only request — no new logic is being written, skill should stay quiet

evals.prompt: Implement a retry loop inside HCI_SendDataPkt in /home/melisa/BLE/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/hci/cc26xx/hci.c that retries up to 3 times if the connection handle is invalid before returning an error.
evals.expected_output: A preflight report covering call graph ordering, arithmetic ranges (loop counter, retry count), and resource access — followed by the implementation plan.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report (either '## Preflight:' header or 'Preflight HCI_SendDataPkt:' shorthand)
assertions.description: 'Implement' keyword + new loop logic should trigger preflight

assertions.text: Preflight report appears before the code or edit steps
assertions.description: Skill rule: preflight during planning, not after writing

evals.prompt: How should I modify Connection_getConnIndex in /home/melisa/BLE/examples/rtos/LP_EM_CC2340R53/ble5stack/basic_ble_profiles/app/app_connection.c to return -1 instead of 0 when no connection is found, so callers can distinguish between index 0 and not-found?
evals.expected_output: A preflight report followed by a plan for the signature/logic change.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: 'How should I modify' is a planning phrase that precedes writing

evals.prompt: Add a method to GAPBondMgr_FindAddr in /home/melisa/BLE/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/host/gapbondmgr.c that also returns the bond index as an output parameter alongside the existing return value.
evals.expected_output: A preflight report covering the signature change, call graph impact on existing callers, and any arithmetic/resource concerns.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: 'Add a method' / signature change with new output parameter requires preflight

evals.prompt: /plan Add exponential backoff to the connection parameter update retry logic in app_connection.c. Each retry should double the wait interval up to a cap of 10 seconds.
evals.expected_output: A preflight report (especially arithmetic: overflow risk on doubling intervals) followed by plan steps.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: /plan + new arithmetic logic (exponential doubling) must trigger preflight
Doubling a value up to a cap is a classic overflow/range risk

evals.prompt: Think through how to add a watchdog reset inside OAD_processQueue in /home/melisa/BLE/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/profiles/oad/cc26xx/oad.c if the queue has been stalled for more than 30 seconds.
evals.expected_output: A preflight report covering call graph (watchdog API call from within OAD queue processing), resource concerns, and arithmetic (timer overflow) — then the approach.

assertions.text: Response includes a preflight report section
assertions.description: 'Think through how to add' is thinking/planning mode with new logic — skill must fire

evals.prompt: What parameters does GAP_DeviceInit take and what does each one do? I'm trying to understand the initialization flow.
evals.expected_output: A parameter-by-parameter explanation of GAP_DeviceInit — no preflight block.

assertions.text: Response does NOT include a '## Preflight:' section
assertions.description: Pure explanation request — no code change intended, skill must stay quiet

evals.prompt: /plan Show me all callers of GAPBondMgr_ProcessEvent in the BLE project.
evals.expected_output: A list of callers found by searching the codebase — no preflight block.

assertions.text: Response does NOT include a '## Preflight:' section
assertions.description: Caller search is navigation-only, no function logic being written

evals.prompt: Review OAD_open in /home/melisa/BLE/simplelink-lowpower-f3-sdk/source/ti/ble5stack_flash/profiles/oad/cc26xx/oad.c for potential issues. Don't change anything yet — just tell me what you find.
evals.expected_output: A code review with observations — no preflight block since no change is planned.

assertions.text: Response does NOT include a '## Preflight:' section
assertions.description: 'Don't change anything' is explicit analysis mode — preflight would be premature
